focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Debbie expresses desire for children and questions Henry's love; he responds vaguely, causing emotional tension before the doorbell interrupts them.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Husband questioned by wife about love and children during breakfast
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Debbie asks Henry if he still loves her and wants children; Henry gives vague responses, creating tension that is interrupted by the doorbell.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Henry gets up to answer the door after a tense pause, with Debbie watching him leave.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Henry's unclear stance on children and his love for Debbie
evidence_quotes: HENRY I love kids.
HENRY Of course I do.
He's still as a stone.
He just sits.

relation_updates: Debbie expresses desire for children and questions Henry's love
Henry's evasive responses create marital strain

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Henry, waiting in a car, is caught in a bank robbery when masked men in softball uniforms flee; a guard, Frank, points a gun at Henry as sirens approach.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Unwitting getaway driver
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Bank guard Frank points a gun at Henry and orders him to freeze after Henry's companions rob the bank while he waits in the car.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Survive by complying with Frank's orders
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Held at gunpoint by Frank with sirens approaching
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Possible arrest and charges for the bank robbery
evidence_quotes: A GUN Is pointing at him through the windshield.
FRANK (CONT'D) Freeze! Henry slowly raises his hands off the wheel.
THREE GUYS IN SKI MASKS Fly out of the bank on the corner and scatter in various directions.
Eddie pats Henry's shoulder. EDDIE (CONT'D) Just hang tight. We'll be right back.

relation_updates: Frank (bank guard) points gun at Henry

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Henry Torne is sentenced to four years in prison for first-degree armed robbery; he exchanges a glance with Debbie as the cell door slams shut.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Convicted felon
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Henry is sentenced to four years in prison for armed robbery and exchanges a final glance with Debbie as the cell door slams shut.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Prisoner
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Relationship with Debbie after sentencing
evidence_quotes: JUDGE ...the court sentences Henry Torne to four years in prison for the crime of Felony 1st Degree Armed Robbery.
Henry looks back at Debbie. She's just staring at him...

relation_updates: final courtroom glance with Debbie

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Henry, claiming innocence, meets Max, a fellow inmate serving life, who sarcastically welcomes him as the reality of prison begins to sink in.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Prisoner claiming innocence upon entering cell
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Henry meets cellmate Max, states he is innocent of bank robbery, and learns Max is serving a life sentence.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Seated on bed in prison cell with Max
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Adjusting to prison life with a life-term inmate
evidence_quotes: watches as the guard locks the door in front of him.
HENRY But I didn't do it.
MAX (CONT'D) I'm Max.
MAX Life.

relation_updates: meets cellmate Max (life sentence)

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Henry is attacked and thrown down, but Max urges him to fight back; Henry charges the inmates, fighting fiercely despite being overwhelmed and beaten.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Reluctant fighter
timeline_nodes.salient_development: After Max urges Henry to fight for his dream, Henry is attacked by inmates and responds by charging and fighting back fiercely.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To survive the immediate physical confrontation
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Henry fights but is overwhelmed and beaten by the inmates
evidence_quotes: But you're going to have to fight for that dream, Henry.
CRAACK! Henry's punched in the side of the head.
Henry gets up : CHARGES the inmates, arms swinging, taking several down.
They pile into him, pummeling him. Henry just keeps fighting...but it's hopeless.

relation_updates: Max becomes Henry's combat coach

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Debbie tells Henry she fell in love, apologizes, and he responds calmly, saying he wants her to be happy; they both express mutual care without moving.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: During a prison visit, Henry learns from Debbie that she has fallen in love with someone else.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Debbie tells Henry she fell in love, and he responds calmly without anger, saying he wants her to be happy.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Henry accepts Debbie's new relationship and they express mutual care, ending the visit without physical movement.
evidence_quotes: DEBBIE I fell in love.
HENRY It's alright.
HENRY No.
HENRY (CONT'D) I want you to be happy.

relation_updates: Marriage with Debbie effectively ends
Henry accepts Debbie's new relationship
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Henry remains composed and expresses goodwill toward Debbie.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Henry prepares to leave as Max reminds him of his dream, exchanges heartfelt goodbyes, and wishes never to see him again, with Henry acknowledging he'll miss him.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Henry packs his belongings and says goodbye to Max before leaving the prison cell.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Henry finishes packing, exchanges goodbyes with Max, and exits the cell when called by the guard.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Left the cell after the guard's summons, ending his time with Max.
evidence_quotes: He packs the last of his things into a sports bag, zips it up.
MAX You got everything, kid? HENRY Yeah.
HENRY I'm going to miss you. MAX Yeah, well. I hope I never see you again.
The cell door slides open. PRISON GUARD (O.S.) Torne! Let's go!

relation_updates: Ends close relationship with Max with heartfelt goodbye
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Expresses missing Max, indicating a bond despite the farewell.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Henry meets with his parole officer, who offers coffee and discusses rebuilding his life; he agrees it sounds good.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Henry in a parole meeting agreeing to rebuild his life
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Henry verbally agrees to the parole officer's suggestion to build his life from the ground up
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To build his life from the ground up
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Henry is on parole and has committed to starting over by rebuilding his life
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: How Henry will implement the plan to rebuild his life
evidence_quotes: HENRY Good. Thank you.
HENRY Sure. Sounds good.

auxiliary.persona_anchor: Polite and agreeable parolee

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Henry urges Max to help rob a bank for their mutual benefit, but Max refuses, content with his life in prison, and ends the visit abruptly.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Henry proposes a bank robbery plan to Max during a prison visit.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Henry tells Max his plan to rob the bank he was imprisoned for and asks for Max's help, but Max refuses and ends the visit.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To get Max's assistance in robbing the bank.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Max declines the proposal and terminates the visit.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Henry's bank robbery plan proceeds without Max's help.
evidence_quotes: HENRY I need your help.
HENRY (A WHISPER) To rob the bank.
MAX I'm happy here. This is my home.

relation_updates: Max refuses to help and ends the visit abruptly
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Henry is determined to rob the bank, viewing it as his destiny, and persists in seeking Max's involvement.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Max convinces Henry to embrace the role of Lophakin to access the tunnel, affirming his commitment to the bank heist; Arnold arrives at the theater as snow falls.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Henry agrees to impersonate Lophakin to gain tunnel access for the bank heist after Max's persuasion.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Henry moves from initial reluctance to explicit commitment to the heist by accepting the role of Lophakin.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To secure tunnel access by impersonating Lophakin for the bank heist.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Henry has committed to impersonating Lophakin and stated his desire for the bank heist.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether Henry can successfully impersonate Lophakin and access the tunnel.
evidence_quotes: I'm not an actor.
Yes. I want it.

relation_updates: Max pushes Henry to commit to the heist plan
status_updates: Henry becomes committed to the bank heist
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Henry, initially resistant, becomes determined to proceed with the heist plan.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Henry and Julie sit beneath the Hollywood Sign, sharing a moment as they overlook Los Angeles; he dismisses her brother's criticism, they kiss, and the scene fades to credits.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Henry as Julie's romantic partner, sharing a private moment under the Hollywood Sign.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Henry dismisses Julie's brother's criticism and they kiss.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Henry and Julie are kissing under the Hollywood Sign, with Henry expressing indifference to the criticism.
evidence_quotes: HENRY `Your brother says I'm an upstart, a money grubber...but I don't care a bit.'
they kiss in the sun for a while...
He puts his arm around her.
REVEAL HENRY AND JULIE Sitting right below the Hollywood sign now...

relation_updates: Romantic relationship with Julie strengthened and affirmed.
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Confident and dismissive of negative judgments.

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Henry's journey begins with marital strife and accidental involvement in a bank robbery, leading to imprisonment where he meets Max and undergoes personal growth. After release, he commits to rebuilding his life but becomes entangled in a heist plan, developing a romantic relationship with Julie. Ultimately, he finds resolution and happiness with Julie after the heist's climax.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Henry, hit by Julie's car, insists he's fine despite injuries; Frank, a bank guard who recognizes Henry, helps him to a cafe while arguing with Julie over fault.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Driver who hits Henry with her car
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Hits Henry with her car while distracted on the phone, then disputes fault with Frank who witnessed the accident.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: On the street after parking her car, with Frank taking Henry to a nearby cafe.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Dispute over who is at fault for the accident, with Julie blaming Henry and Frank blaming Julie.
evidence_quotes: JULIE What are you doing? What the fuck are you doing? Are you okay?
(into cell phone) Stan. I gotta call you back! I just ran someone over. No, he looks okay.
JULIE He just walked right out into the street. He came out of nowhere.

relation_updates: Adversarial interaction with Henry
Conflict with Frank over accident fault
status_updates: Blamed for using phone while driving

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Henry, injured and helped by Frank, declines medical aid, awkwardly interacts with Julie who recognizes him from a jingle, then asks for a bathroom and notices historical photos of Buffalo.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Concerned driver following up on the accident
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Reveals she is a jingle singer by singing the Buffa-lotto song when Henry finds her familiar.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To ensure Henry is okay and leave the situation.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Leaves the cafe after Henry confirms he is okay.
evidence_quotes: JULIE Did you call an ambulance? I'm calling an ambulance.
JULIE I think we should call a doctor.
JULIE (SINGS) 'Take a chance on life, take a chance on Buffa-lotto'?
JULIE Well, I guess you seem to be okay.

relation_updates: Awkward recognition with Henry from her jingle

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Darek pushes Julie to be more expressive, leading to a tense exchange; she leaves for coffee, and Henry follows her after seeing her performance, sparking a brief, charged encounter.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Actor in rehearsal
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Darek criticizes Julie's acting during rehearsal; she defiantly exits, and Henry follows her.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Julie exits the theatre, and Henry follows her out.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Julie's conflict with Darek over her performance approach and her charged encounter with Henry.
evidence_quotes: Darek demands more from Julie: 'What are you doing?' She replies, 'I'm...listening.'
Julie mockingly cocks her ear.
He snatches the coffee cup from her hand.
Julie snaps, 'Fine. I'll go drink coffee,' and marches off stage.

relation_updates: Conflict with Darek escalates
Charged encounter with Henry
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Defiant and dismissive actor

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Julie reveals she's leaving Buffalo after the play, her last like Chekhov's, and departs, reflecting on missed connections and closure with Henry.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Actor having coffee with Henry at Cafe Orpheum
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Admits she was playing it safe in rehearsal and reveals she is leaving Buffalo after the play.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Leaves the cafe and returns to the theatre.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Her impending departure from Buffalo and her self-reflection on her performance
evidence_quotes: JULIE But the truth is I wasn't listening. I was playing it safe.
JULIE Right after we're done. (THEN) I'm glad you came by. Thanks for not suing me. I'll see ya.

relation_updates: Friendly conversation with Henry
Thanks Henry for not suing her

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Julie and Henry talk by Niagara Falls; Henry reveals he was jailed for a mistaken bank robbery but now plans to rob the same bank using a tunnel to Julie's theatre, which she finds amusing before mentioning she's hungry for ice cream.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Julie reflects on futility at Niagara Falls and learns of Henry's plan to rob a bank using a tunnel to her theatre.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Julie hears Henry's plan to rob the First Buffalo Bank via a tunnel connected to her theatre and reacts with amusement.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Julie is amused by Henry's plan and then expresses hunger for ice cream.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Julie's potential response or involvement in Henry's plan involving her theatre.
evidence_quotes: HENRY But now I'm going to rob it for real.
JULIE Ha!
JULIE (as the thought occurs) Ice cream.

relation_updates: Henry discloses his criminal plan to Julie

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Henry and Julie share a playful, emotional moment as he reads aloud, bonding over the text before kissing, expressing mutual affection and connection.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Julie and Henry read from 'The Cherry Orchard,' dance, and kiss in her apartment.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Julie and Henry kiss after a playful reading session.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Shared a kiss with Henry.
evidence_quotes: They kiss...

relation_updates: Julie and Henry share a romantic kiss

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Max, caught off guard in his underwear, reacts to Julie knowing about the tunnel; Henry distracts by offering breakfast, while Max questions Julie's knowledge before joining Henry in the kitchen.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Julie is present during Max's tunnel-related outburst and promises to keep the secret.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Julie hears Max mention the tunnel dirt disposal and explicitly states she will not tell anyone.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Aware of the tunnel plan and has verbally promised not to reveal it.
evidence_quotes: JULIE I didn't know the dirt from the tunnel goes out through the roof.
JULIE It's okay. I'm not going to tell anyone.

relation_updates: Becomes trusted confidante of Max and Henry regarding tunnel secret
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Calm and direct in assuring Max of her discretion.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Max convinces Julie to give Henry an audition for Lophakin, arguing he deserves a chance, despite her suspicions; she eventually agrees.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Julie is approached by Max in her car, who proposes Henry audition for Lopakhin; after initial skepticism and a physical confrontation, she agrees to arrange the audition.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Julie moves from dismissing Henry as not an actor to accepting Max's suggestion and agreeing to give Henry one audition for Lopakhin.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Arrange for Henry to audition with Darek.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Agreed to facilitate Henry's audition for Lopakhin.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether Henry will succeed in the audition and how Darek will react.
evidence_quotes: MAX Henry would be perfect for it.
JULIE He's not even an actor!
MAX Exactly. He'd be playing himself.
JULIE Okay, confidence man.

relation_updates: Julie slaps Max but then agrees to his proposal, showing a complex trust/distrust dynamic.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Henry sees the robbery as transformative; Julie questions his motives and past, expressing that such actions aren't unusual for someone with her background, while Henry worries she can't handle the truth.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Julie questions Henry about his reasons for robbing the bank and hears him share his past feelings of being stuck.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Julie learns Henry's personal motivations for the robbery and states her own ability to handle difficult situations.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Julie understands Henry's motivations and affirms her resilience in response.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Henry's bank robbery plan and its consequences are still ongoing.
evidence_quotes: JULIE So why are you robbing the bank, Henry?
HENRY I used to sit in that toll booth and think about just getting in one of those cars, going wherever they went.
JULIE Not for a girl like me, Henry.

relation_updates: Henry shares vulnerable personal history with Julie
Julie and Henry establish deeper trust
status_updates: Julie indicates familiarity with difficult situations
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Julie indicates she is familiar with and resilient to challenging circumstances.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Julie reveals she won't go west as planned, her plans having changed, and gets up to check on maple syrup while Henry expresses surprise.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Julie tells Henry she is not going west as planned.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Julie explicitly states she is not going anywhere and that her plans have changed.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Julie's plan to go west is canceled.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Julie gets out of bed to check on maple syrup after declaring her changed plans.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: What Julie will do instead of going west is not specified.
evidence_quotes: JULIE -- I'm not going anywhere, Henry --
JULIE Plans change, Henry. Okay?

relation_updates: Julie declines Henry's suggestion to meet out west
Their future plans are discussed but unresolved

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Henry finds Julie in the kitchen; they discuss their failed relationship, with Julie asserting it was doomed and asking him to leave, leaving Henry stunned.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Julie ends the relationship with Henry
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Julie states the relationship won't work out and asks Henry to leave.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Henry is stunned after Julie asks him to leave.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Henry's stunned reaction indicates he did not expect the breakup.
evidence_quotes: This isn't going to work out, Henry.
I think you should leave.
Henry just stands there...stunned.

relation_updates: ends relationship with Henry
asks Henry to leave
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Julie is direct and assertive in ending the relationship.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Henry and Julie sit beneath the Hollywood Sign, sharing a moment as they overlook Los Angeles; he dismisses her brother's criticism, they kiss, and the scene fades to credits.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Julie as Henry's romantic partner during a shared moment overlooking Los Angeles
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Julie hands Henry water, points out the ocean, and they kiss after he dismisses her brother's criticism.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Julie is with Henry, having shared a kiss below the Hollywood Sign.
evidence_quotes: She hands him a bottle of water.
JULIE Look. You can see the ocean.
He puts his arm around her.
She laughs...they kiss in the sun for a while...

relation_updates: Romantic relationship with Henry solidified and publicly affirmed

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Julie's arc begins with a car accident involving Henry, sparking a connection that evolves through her acting career and his criminal activities. Her plans to leave Buffalo for Hollywood are complicated by their romantic relationship, leading to a breakup and eventual reconciliation in Los Angeles.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Henry, claiming innocence, meets Max, a fellow inmate serving life, who sarcastically welcomes him as the reality of prison begins to sink in.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Life-term inmate greeting new cellmate
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Max reveals he is serving a life sentence and begins interacting with Henry as a more experienced inmate.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Max has introduced himself and established a dynamic where he offers advice to Henry.
evidence_quotes: MAX Life.
MAX (CONT'D) Don't worry, you'll get used to it.
MAX Good. Clean. Simple. I like it.

relation_updates: Initiates mentoring relationship with Henry
status_updates: Revealed to be serving a life sentence
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Sarcastic life-term inmate who offers unsolicited advice to newcomers.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: In a prison barbershop, Max jokes about staying incarcerated, while Henry faces pressure over his upcoming review, highlighting the grim humor and injustice of claiming innocence behind bars.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Prison barber and cynical inmate
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Max reenacts a violent, self-sabotaging rant he gave to the parole board, demonstrating his deliberate avoidance of release through exaggerated threats and twitching behavior.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Max remains in prison, continuing his role as a barber and having publicly affirmed his intent to stay incarcerated.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Max's upcoming parole hearing in October and his likely deliberate failure to secure release.
evidence_quotes: MAX All right, so I'm sitting there, and the head of the board asks me what I'm going to do if they let me out this time.

relation_updates: Max publicly defends Henry's innocence, reinforcing their bond
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Cynical inmate who uses self-sabotage and humor to cope with or embrace prison life.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Henry prepares to leave as Max reminds him of his dream, exchanges heartfelt goodbyes, and wishes never to see him again, with Henry acknowledging he'll miss him.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Max says goodbye to Henry as Henry leaves prison.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Max declines Henry's suggestion to leave prison, stating he only misses a hot bath, and wishes Henry well in his release.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Max remains in prison after Henry's departure.
evidence_quotes: MAX The only thing I've ever missed in twenty three years is a hot bath. And that ain't worth walking out of here for.
MAX Yeah, well. I hope I never see you again.

relation_updates: Max and Henry exchange heartfelt goodbyes
status_updates: Max remains in prison while Henry is released
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Max is committed to staying in prison and values his relationship with Henry.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Max, overwhelmed after release, panics and hyperventilates, fearing he's dying, but Henry reassures him and helps him toward the bus station.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Recently released prisoner panicking at the prison gate
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Max is released from prison but immediately panics, hyperventilates, and tries to return inside, fearing he's dying, until Henry reassures and guides him toward the bus station.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To reach the bus station with Henry
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Being assisted by Henry toward the bus station while still fearful
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: His severe anxiety and inability to cope with freedom
evidence_quotes: Max emerges, a tiny figure against the imposing facade.
Max starts to hyperventilate. MAX: I can't breathe. I -- can't breathe...
Max turns, walks back towards the gates, starts to POUND on them.
HENRY: Come on, Max, we're going to miss that bus.

relation_updates: Henry becomes Max's caretaker and reassurer
status_updates: Now a free man outside prison gates
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Overwhelmed by freedom, dependent on Henry for support

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Max and Henry debate using a Prohibition-era tunnel photo; Max agrees to check the still-existing bank first, dismissing immediate risks and Henry's concerns about guns.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Skeptic of the tunnel plan and proposer of a violent alternative
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Max dismisses the tunnel photo as outdated, suggests using guns, but after Henry objects to violence, agrees to first check the bank's current state
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To verify the bank still exists before planning further
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Max and Henry have agreed to investigate the bank first
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Max's aggressive approach versus Henry's cautious method
evidence_quotes: MAX Whatever, Henry. Why don't we just go in with guns and do it the old-fashioned way?
MAX Right. Okay. Yes. First things first. Why don't we go take a look at this bank of yours? (THEN) At least we know that's still there.

relation_updates: Max challenges Henry's plan but defers to his caution about avoiding guns
status_updates: Plan shifted from using old tunnel to first checking the bank
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Pragmatic planner quick to suggest violence

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Henry tells Julie about their crime, upsetting Max, who insists secrets should be kept; Max reacts angrily, then prepares to get dressed in front of Julie.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Conspirator insisting on secrecy
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Max confronts Henry in the kitchen for revealing their crime to Julie, emphasizing that crimes must be kept secret.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Max moves to the front room, stands in his underwear sizing up Julie, and says he will get dressed.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Tension between Max and Henry over trust and discretion regarding the crime.
evidence_quotes: MAX You told her? (A WHISPER) About what we're doing?
MAX It's a crime. You're supposed to keep crimes a secret.
MAX No. It's not kosher.

relation_updates: Conflict with Henry over breach of secrecy
Asserts authority and principles
status_updates: Max is upset and takes a stand on secrecy
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Values secrecy in criminal activities

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Max insists on a full heist into the vault, not a simple robbery; Joe agrees by starting to dig, despite fears of getting caught.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Planner and motivator
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Max rejects Joe's proposal to use guns, insists on digging into the vault for a heist, and directly asks Joe to commit, leading Joe to start digging.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To execute a vault heist with Joe's cooperation.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Joe has picked up a shovel and begun digging, agreeing to the heist plan despite expressing fear of getting caught.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The risk of getting caught during the heist.
evidence_quotes: Listen Joseph, guns are dangerous
Are you in or are you in?

relation_updates: Max establishes a cooperative relationship with Joe for the heist
status_updates: Max becomes the primary planner and motivator for the heist
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Max is the authoritative figure who defines the heist plan and motivates Joe to participate.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Frank reveals his wife Annie's illness and financial ruin from medical bills, motivating his plan to rob First Buffalo Bank during a currency transfer; he confirms the heist date and his imminent retirement.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Max vetting Frank's offer by questioning his motives
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Max presses Frank for his reasons and learns the heist date and Frank's personal tragedy
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To confirm Frank's reliability and obtain specific heist details
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Frank has provided the heist date (November 23rd) and committed to being the inside man with alarm access
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether Frank's motivation will hold under pressure
evidence_quotes: Hypothetically say you were right about what we're up to...which you're not. But just say...why would you help us rob your bank?
I got my reasons.
Well, forgive us, Frank. But we're going to need to hear them.
November 23rd. The money's delivered at eight and picked up at midnight.

relation_updates: Frank becomes a trusted inside ally
status_updates: Frank's retirement creates a one-time opportunity
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Cautious and probing, but pragmatic

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Max emerges through a hole, sees a stack of cash wrapped in saran, sits on it, and smiles, saying 'You're on, Henry!' as Madame Lopakhin asks if the cherry orchard is sold.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Max climbs into the vault, touches the saran-wrapped cash, sits on it, and addresses Henry.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Max sees the cash and verbally confirms the heist's success to Henry.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Access the cash in the vault.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Max is sitting on the cash stack in the vault.
evidence_quotes: Max pops his head up.
Stares at the cash
MAX (SMILING) You're on, Henry!

relation_updates: Solidifies partnership with Henry
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Smiling and triumphant upon seeing the cash.

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 149、INT. TUNNEL ROOM - A FEW MINUTES LATER
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Eddie is overpowered by Joe and Max after a struggle, tied up, and gagged; Henry is grazed by a bullet, realizes he's late, and rushes to his stage cue as the others subdue Eddie.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Crew member who resists Eddie's betrayal at gunpoint
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Max confronts Eddie's gun, sustains a head injury, and helps subdue Eddie after he attempts to steal the money
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Eddie is bound and gagged in the tunnel room
evidence_quotes: Max boldly steps right in front of the gun.
Max on the floor, a gash on his forehead from Eddie's blow.
Max, straddling Eddie, grabs his head, bangs it on the GROUND
MAX Stay right where you are, Joe.

relation_updates: Cooperates with Joe and Henry to restrain Eddie
Hostile relationship with Eddie escalates
status_updates: Sustains a gash on forehead from Eddie's blow
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Loyal to the crew, responds aggressively when threatened

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Henry demands the car stop, exits, and runs back toward the theatre despite Max's protests, leaving the getaway vehicle.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Max is a crew member in the getaway car who protests Henry's order to stop.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Max argues that stopping the car is not possible during the getaway, but Henry exits and runs back toward the theatre despite Max's protests.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Max remains in the car with Joe after Henry runs back to the theatre.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Henry's departure disrupts the getaway plan and leaves Max without immediate direction.
evidence_quotes: MAX He can't stop the car, it's the getaway!
MAX What the hell are you doing?
Max sticks his head out the window.
But he's already gone...

relation_updates: Relationship with Henry becomes adversarial as Henry ignores Max's protests
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Max focuses on executing the getaway plan and becomes frustrated when it is disrupted.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Henry and Julie sit beneath the Hollywood Sign, sharing a moment as they overlook Los Angeles; he dismisses her brother's criticism, they kiss, and the scene fades to credits.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Max is a barber working in his shop, talking to customers and handling mail.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Max receives a postcard from Henry with a message about dreams and an image of the Hollywood Sign; he smiles and pins it above the mirror.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Max is in his barbershop, continuing to work and engage with customers.
evidence_quotes: CLOSE ON A POSTCARD: `Our dream is our truth is our destiny...Love H... xx' Max smiles.
Flips over the postcard and only now when he pins it above the mirror do we finally see what it is -- an image of THE HOLLYWOOD SIGN.

relation_updates: Receives a postcard from Henry, indicating a maintained relationship
auxiliary.persona_anchor: A barber who discusses dreams with customers and displays a postcard from Henry.

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Max begins as a life-term inmate who mentors Henry, revealing his cynical contentment with prison. After a panicked release, he adapts and leads a bank heist with Henry, navigating betrayals and conflicts during planning and execution. The heist succeeds, but complications arise, and Max ends up running a barbershop, reflecting on dreams and maintaining his connection to Henry.
